blocks.content: Larry found a table near the back wall. Not isolated. Not obvious. Somewhere in the middle of nowhere.

The cafeteria noise washed over him — a hundred conversations he wasn't part of. He watched a freshman at the next table try to say something funny and get half a laugh. He watched a girl walk up to a table and get moved to the end without anyone meaning anything by it.
He watched Jonathan hold court at the center table like he'd been the center of something his entire life. Which he had.
Halfway through lunch Jonathan looked up and scanned the cafeteria. His eyes moved across the room — past the back wall, past the table where Larry was sitting.
He couldn't tell if Jonathan didn't see him. Or if he did and it didn't register. He wasn't sure which one he was supposed to hope for.
